reviews.review_msg: I thought Bob was awesome and he probably had a greater impact on me than any other professor in eight years of University (160 credits earned in five years at SFU).  I see negative comments.  The thing about numerical analysis is that it builds on calculus and algebra but it really is a different mindset from other math classes.
The course is easy and the exams are fair. He's helpful but his lecture is a little bit boring.

He's a great guy who simply cannot lecture well. Work hard to understand the concepts in the textbook--there's a lot of material in this class, and he's not afraid to test you on it (not just applying but understanding concepts). His exams are tough, but you get a formula sheet and scaling is generous.
He is a good teacher... Its just what he teaches is too complex and intense. can not blame him

This prof is not that bad as people think. He knows his stuff well, but doesn't know how to teach. The course itself is really hard, His exams are killer as well, but if you study hard and clear your concepts, you can do really well in this course.

His notes are no different than the text book.Never actually explain how to do assignment problems with computer(u've to learn to program urself). He sometimes will ask "how many is following me?" and only less than 5-8 students raise hand out of 100 students. His exams are difficult. no cheat sheets,open book and formula on the back of the test.

HE GIVES THE HARDEST EXAMS!!! DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS IF YOU WANT TO RECEIVE A HIGH GRADE. ALL HIS LECTURE MATERIALS ARE FROM THE TEXTBOOK SO LEARNING FROM THE TEXT IS SAME THING AS GOING TO HIS CLASS. HORRIBLE CLASS!!!

HORRIBLE PROFESSOR! He mumbles all the time and does not explain the concepts properly. The exams are ridiculously difficult. He does not provide the solutions to sample exams and assignments. Hence, there is no way to know what the correct answer is. He mumbles all the time. This is my last sem at SFU and Bob Russel is the worst Prof at SFU!!
Just don't take this class with him.exams are extremely hard :(
